title: Managing Context Window Costs
concept: Longer prompts increase latency and API token costs; optimizing prompt size directly improves application performance.

options.text: The limit of text an AI can process at once
A physical glass window in the AI server room
The price of an AI subscription

exercises.explain: The context window is the maximum limit of tokens (text) an AI can read and write at one time.

cards.title: The Hidden Cost of Words
cards.body: Did you know that AI models charge you for every single word they read? If you write bloated prompts, your application will run slow and drain your wallet. Let's learn how to keep your prompts lean and mean!

cards.title: What is Context Cost?
cards.body: Every word, character, and image you send to an AI is measured in units called tokens. The 'context window' is the total amount of tokens the AI can process at once. The more tokens you send inside this window, the more money you pay and the longer you have to wait for a response.

cards.title: The Moving Truck
cards.body: Think of an LLM's context window like a moving truck. If you pack the truck with unnecessary boxes (useless tokens), it moves slower, drinks more fuel, and costs you way more money to rent. By packing only what you need, you get your delivery faster and cheaper.

cards.title: Trimming the Fat
cards.body: Instead of sending an entire 10-page user manual to get a simple answer, you can use a search tool to find the exact 2 paragraphs needed and send only those to the AI. This saves thousands of tokens!

cards.title: Myth: Bigger is Always Better
cards.body: Many think that because modern AI models can read giant books of text at once, they should send everything. In reality, sending massive prompts makes the AI slower to reply and significantly increases your API bill.

cards.title: Fat vs. Fit Prompts
cards.body: Look at how prompt size changes your costs and response times. Keeping things concise makes a huge difference!

left.text: High cost, slow response times, wastes budget on filler words.

right.text: Low cost, fast response times, contains only essential details.

exercises.prompt: What is a common side effect of sending a huge prompt that fills up the context window?

options.text: The AI takes longer to respond (higher latency).
The AI gets much faster at replying.
The price of the API drops to zero.
The AI stops reading the prompt entirely.

exercises.explain: A larger prompt takes longer for the AI to read and process, which increases latency (delay) before you get a reply.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Optimizing your prompt size is a great way to lower your AI application's operating costs.
exercises.explain: Keeping your prompts short and relevant saves tokens, which directly lowers the price you pay per API request.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the rule about AI costs.
exercises.sentence: Sending fewer prompt tokens directly reduces your bill because AI providers charge by the number of processed.
exercises.explain: AI providers charge you based on the number of tokens (words or word pieces) processed by the model. Keep them low to save money.

exercises.prompt: Which of these prompts shows an developer trying to optimize the context window?

options.text: Here is a detailed, multi-paragraph history of our company dating back to 1998...
Summarize the following text in under 10 words: [Text]
Let's chat about your day! Tell me absolutely everything that happened.

exercises.explain: Prompt B is a clear attempt by an AI to stay brief and reduce token usage, while A and C are verbose human-like outputs.

exercises.prompt: You are building an AI customer support bot that will run thousands of times a day. Which strategy will keep your API bills low and response times fast?

options.text: Send the entire database of past chat logs to ensure accuracy.
Use concise prompts and trim down example data to only what is needed.
Add extra conversational filler to make the AI feel more human.
Switch to a model with a larger context window and send everything.

exercises.explain: To keep cost and latency low, you should only include essential instructions and trim down the example data. Sending the entire historical log is a waste of context window tokens.
